input.prompt:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

data.content:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

input.initialPrompt:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

data.reasoningText:
The user has migrated from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP) and is hitting exit code 8 when test assemblies produce zero tests. This is a known behavioral difference — MTP returns a non-zero exit code when no tests are discovered, whereas VSTest treated that as success.

Let me look at the files in the workspace to understand their setup, then provide the fix.

data.content:
**Exit code 8 = "no tests discovered."** MTP treats zero-test assemblies as failures, unlike VSTest which silently succeeded.

You have two options to fix this:

### Option 1 — Per-run (CI pipeline flag)

Pass `--ignore-exit-code 8` to your `dotnet test` command:

```bash
dotnet test --ignore-exit-code 8
```

This tells MTP to treat "no tests found" as success.

### Option 2 — Project-level (recommended for your case)

Set an environment variable or MSBuild property so it's always applied. Add this to your `.csproj` or `Directory.Build.props`:

arguments.new_str:
<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Treat "no tests discovered" (exit code 8) as success for conditionally-compiled assemblies -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>

<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Treat "no tests discovered" (exit code 8) as success for conditionally-compiled assemblies -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>

data.content:
**Fixed.** The exit code is now `0` even with zero tests discovered.

### Summary

| Aspect | VSTest | MTP |
|---|---|---|
| Zero tests found | Exit code 0 (silent) | Exit code 8 (failure) |

The `--ignore-exit-code 8` flag (set via `<TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>` in the `.csproj`) restores the VSTest behavior. You can alternatively set the environment variable `TESTINGPLATFORM_EXITCODE_IGNORE=8` in your CI pipeline, or pass `--ignore-exit-code 8` directly on the `dotnet test` command line.
